reasoning: No US President has ever been removed from power via Section 4 of the 25th Amendment. Section 4 requires the Vice President and a majority of the Cabinet to actively declare the President incapacitated. If the President contests this, it requires a two-thirds vote in both the House and the Senate to uphold the declaration. With Republicans controlling the Senate (53 seats) and Trump having selected historically loyal Cabinet members and Vice President, there is practically a 0% chance of a successful Section 4 invocation by his own administration by the end of 2025. Current discussions regarding the 25th Amendment are entirely driven by partisan opponents (e.g., Democrats like Governor JB Pritzker and Maxine Waters) over political disagreements, which have absolutely no authority to invoke Section 4. The Polymarket price of 2% largely represents the absolute noise floor for prediction markets rather than a true likelihood. The probability is safely placed at the minimum allowed API boundary.
